steps.title: Read Jack's sarcasm
steps.content: Jack responds sarcastically to Bartley's complaint about rain. He says that if rain did not come, Bartley would carry a leaky pail on his head so he would still have a reason to complain. The joke targets Bartley's complaining habit.

steps.title: Infer Jack's belief
steps.content: Choice A invents the idea that Bartley will help cut hay. Choice B shifts the concern to Jack's preparation, even though Bartley complains about rain falling on himself. Choice C says Bartley misjudges weather, but Jack does not challenge the forecast. Jack implies Bartley will find something wrong in any circumstances. The answer is D.
